title: Satay Sweet Potato Soup
description: A warming sweet potato and red lentil soup with peanut butter, ginger and chilli, topped with crisp potato peelings and lime.
context: Look for peanut butter without added palm oil, salt or sugar.
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Scatter the sweet potato peelings on a baking tray. Toss with 1 tbsp of the oil and some seasoning. Cook for 15–20 minutes until golden and crisp.

Heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil in a large pan and fry onions and carrots for 5 minutes until beginning to soften. Add garlic, ginger and most of the chilli, stirring for 1 minute. Add sweet potato chunks, tomatoes, lentils, stock and lime juice. Bring to the boil, then simmer for 20 minutes or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Add the peanut butter. For a chunky texture, mash the soup. For a smooth texture, blend in batches then return to the pan. Check seasoning, adding water to loosen if needed.
Reheat if necessary. Ladle into warm bowls and garnish with remaining chilli and peanuts. Serve with the crisp potato peelings and lime wedges.
